name: The Hitchin Triathlon
location: Butts Close / Hitchin Outdoor Pool, Fishponds Road, Hitchin
tagline: Sprint · Nitro · Nano · Atom — 4 distances for all ages
summary: BurntHare's flagship multisport event returns to Hitchin's much-loved outdoor pool. Starting and finishing at Butts Close, the Triathlon offers four distances so the whole family can take part on the same day, from first-timers to seasoned age-groupers.

course.description: An open-water-style pool swim at Hitchin Outdoor Pool, followed by a closed-road bike loop out towards the surrounding villages, finishing with a flat, fast run back into Butts Close. Fully marshalled throughout with mile markers on the run.

maps.label: Run route — 2.5km lap (run twice for the Sprint)

notes: Entry includes timing chip, event t-shirt and finisher's medal
Bikes can be racked from 6:30am on race day — overnight secure racking also available the night before
Body marking required for all adults — registration and transition close at 6:20am

rules: British Triathlon race rules apply to the Sprint and Nitro distances
Helmets must be worn and fastened at all times while on the bike, including in transition
No drafting on the bike course for the Sprint distance
Children's races (Nano/Atom) must be accompanied to registration by a parent or guardian
Headphones are not permitted on any race distance
Strictly no members of the public allowed inside transition
prizes: Medal for every finisher, every distance
Event t-shirt included in all entries
Trophies for 1st, 2nd and 3rd overall (male and female) in the Sprint and Nitro
Age-category prizes across all adult distances
Special finisher certificates for all Nano and Atom participants

locationDetails.address: Butts Close, Fishponds Road, Hitchin, Hertfordshire, SG5 1PT
locationDetails.parking: Parking is available at the Hitchin Swimming Pool (paid and display — usually free on Sundays). There are other free spaces nearby if you know where to look, and it's a short walk from Hitchin station.
locationDetails.mapQuery: Hitchin Outdoor Pool, Fishponds Road, Hitchin

name: Hitchin Town Centre 5km
summary: Now in its 10th year, the Hitchin Town Centre 5km is BurntHare's most popular event — a fast, flat course through the heart of Hitchin, taking in the historic market square, the riverside path and the iconic steel statues by Cooper's Yard.

course.description: Two 2.5km laps through Hitchin town centre, fully marked and marshalled. Starting on the High Street, the course weaves across the river and heads up past Windmill Hill, before looping around onto Payne's Park and down towards the start.

maps.label: 5km route — two laps of the town centre

registration.feeNote: Entry fees vary — see the booking page for current pricing
registration.deadline: Online entries close the week of the race
notes: Event t-shirt available as additional cost

rules: UK Athletics rules apply
No dogs, buggies or cycles on the course
No headphones for safety on the closed-road sections
prizes: Medal for every finisher
Event t-shirt available as additional cost
Trophies for 1st, 2nd and 3rd overall (male and female)
Age-category prizes in 10-year bands

locationDetails.address: Hitchin High Street, Hitchin, Hertfordshire, SG5 1AY
locationDetails.parking: Town centre car parks (Queen Street, Portmill Lane, Hollow Lane) are a short walk away. Please don't park along the race route, and parking is usually limited so consider car-sharing or public transport.

name: The Hitchin 10km
summary: Welcome to The Hitchin 10km, which also incorporates the Herts County Senior Championships. It's the only 10km in the region that starts and finishes in a market square — a beautiful route out towards Gosmore and Charlton villages before that famous Town Centre finish, paired with the ever-popular Children's Golden Mile.

course.description: Starting right in the town centre, the first kilometre (the north loop) is on closed roads going anti-clockwise around town. Runners then head out towards Gosmore and Charlton to complete a clockwise south loop, before heading back into Hitchin for another 1km loop and that famous Town Centre finish. Classified flat, with one small climb.

maps.label: 10km route — 10.00km, Hitchin out to Gosmore and Charlton

registration.feeNote: 10km and Children's Golden Mile — see the booking page for current pricing
registration.deadline: Online entries close the week of the race
notes: The minimum age for the 10km is 15 on race day — there's a Golden Mile for ages 5–14
No music players permitted due to road crossings on the course

rules: UK Athletics rules apply to the 10km
Minimum age of race entry is 15 on race day; Golden Mile is for ages 5–14
This race is not traffic-free — listen to marshals at all times, especially at road crossings
No music players are permitted due to the nature of the course
prizes: Medal for every 10km finisher
Trophies for 1st, 2nd and 3rd overall (male and female)
Every Golden Mile finisher receives a certificate and small prize

locationDetails.parking: No on-site parking, but there's a car park across the road. Park & stride is recommended — town centre car parks fill up quickly on race morning.

name: The Great Herts AONB
location: Hertfordshire Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty
summary: Brand new for 2027 — a marathon, half marathon and 10km through the Hertfordshire Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty, with every distance open to runners and cyclists alike. Choose your distance, then choose how you cover it.

course.description: A point-to-point route through protected Hertfordshire countryside, with off-road sections suitable for both running and gravel/mountain bikes. Feed stations roughly every 8km. Shorter distances are subsets of the full marathon route.

registration.feeNote: From £22 (10km) to £48 (Marathon) — same price whether running or riding
notes: Choose your discipline (run or ride) when you enter — separate results and prizes for each
Bikes must be suitable for off-road/gravel terrain; no road bikes
Coach transfer back to the start available for point-to-point finishers

rules: This is a multi-terrain trail/gravel event — appropriate footwear or tyres are required
Riders must wear a helmet at all times on the course
Cut-off times apply on the Marathon distance for both disciplines
Respect the AONB: no littering, stay on marked routes
prizes: Medal for every finisher, every distance, every discipline
Separate trophies for top 3 runners and top 3 riders per distance
Finisher t-shirt for all Marathon and Half entrants

locationDetails.address: Start: Gosmore area, Hertfordshire (exact start venue confirmed closer to the date)
locationDetails.parking: Event parking and signage will be confirmed nearer the date — coach transfers provided for the point-to-point finish.

name: Hitchin Triathlon 2027
location: Butts Close / Hitchin Outdoor Pool, Fishponds Road, Hitchin
tagline: Olympic - Sprint - Nitro - Nano - Atom — 5 distances for all ages
summary: BurntHare's flagship multisport event returns to Hitchin's much-loved outdoor pool. Starting and finishing at Butts Close, the Triathlon offers four distances so the whole family can take part on the same day, from first-timers to seasoned age-groupers.  Now also including an 800 meter pool based Aussie style exit and entrance into the pool Olympic / standard distance triathlon as well

course.description: An open-water-style pool swim at Hitchin Outdoor Pool, followed by a closed-road bike loop out towards the surrounding villages, finishing with a flat, fast run back into Butts Close. Fully marshalled throughout with mile markers on the run.  The olympic distance is two laps of the cycle and four laps of the run.

maps.label: Run route — 2.5km lap (run twice for the Sprint)

registration.deadline: Online entries close one week before the event
notes: Entry includes timing chip, event t-shirt and finisher's medal
Bikes can be racked from 6:30am on race day — overnight secure racking also available the night before
Body marking required for all adults — registration and transition close at 6:20am

rules: British Triathlon race rules apply to the Sprint and Nitro distances
Helmets must be worn and fastened at all times while on the bike, including in transition
No drafting on the bike course for the Sprint distance
Children's races (Nano/Atom) must be accompanied to registration by a parent or guardian
Headphones are not permitted on any race distance
Strictly no members of the public allowed inside transition
prizes: Medal for every finisher, every distance
Event t-shirt included in all entries
Trophies for 1st, 2nd and 3rd overall (male and female) in the Sprint and Nitro
Age-category prizes across all adult distances
Special finisher certificates for all Nano and Atom participants

locationDetails.address: Butts Close, Fishponds Road, Hitchin, Hertfordshire, SG5 1PT
locationDetails.parking: Parking is available at the Hitchin Swimming Pool (paid and display — usually free on Sundays). There are other free spaces nearby if you know where to look, and it's a short walk from Hitchin station.
locationDetails.mapQuery: Hitchin Outdoor Pool, Fishponds Road, Hitchin
